reasoning: Peer set (helmets, axes, arrows, spears) operates in a neutral-to-warm palette: grays, silvers, browns, golds. Candidate sword_iron_v00 presents a saturated cool blue/cyan blade with a bright highlight streak — zero overlap with the peer warm-neutral palette. (1) Outline weight: acceptable 1-2px dark outline, consistent with peers. (2) Outline color: dark near-black, consistent. (3) Shadow direction: indeterminate at this resolution; no clear conflict. (4) Shadow count: single shadow layer apparent, consistent with peers. (5) Highlight intensity: candidate highlight is vivid and saturated vs. peers' subdued sheen — notable break. (6) Color temperature: FAIL — peers are neutral/warm, candidate is distinctly cool. Largest single defect. (7) Detail density: roughly comparable pixel density, acceptable. (8) Proportion: at matching scale sword reads as appropriately sized item; no penalty for item-type size difference. (9) Rendering style: pixel art consistent with Denzi family conventions. (10) Palette overlap: FAIL — candidate blue/cyan appears nowhere in peer palette; minimal overlap. (11) Charm level: candidate retains cute pixel-art appeal, consistent with peer charm register. Summary: two hard failures (color temperature, palette overlap) plus one soft failure (highlight intensity). Iron sword should read gray-silver not bright blue; candidate currently reads as crystal or magic sword, breaking material authenticity within the family.
